Gdelt._comment: GDELT DOC 2.0 news collector (third-party attention). Only read when 'news' is in Collectors. Timespan is a GDELT window token (1w/2w/1m). GDELT allows ~1 request/5s per IP, so the collector is strictly sequential and paces requests by InterRequestDelaySeconds; the value is only validated non-negative, but 6+ is recommended to stay under that limit (the default is 6). On HTTP 429 the reader retries MaxRetriesOn429 times with exponential backoff starting at RetryBackoffSeconds (60 -> 60s/120s).

News._comment: Google News RSS news-attention collector (third-party attention), a DISTINCT kind from the GDELT 'news' collector. Only read when 'newssearch' is in Collectors. Unlike GDELT, Google News RSS is NOT per-IP throttled (verified: back-to-back keyless requests succeed with no key/User-Agent), so the collector is strictly sequential but only paces requests politely by InterRequestDelaySeconds (default 1). MaxRecordsPerCompany caps surviving articles per company; EnglishOnly pins en-US coverage. Producing NewsArticle evidence lifts AttentionScore via spec 70's MediaAttention extraction with no scoring change.

Ai._comment: AI chat-client seam (Microsoft.Extensions.AI IChatClient) + the filing analyzer + the directional filing signal source that consume it. DISABLED by default: a blank Provider means no AI is wired and no provider packages load, so no earnings release is ever read and the deterministic Neutral GuidanceChange (spec 57) stands. Directional filing signals (a beat lifts Trajectory via a Positive GuidanceChange, a miss lowers it via Negative) require AI ENABLED via either: Ollama (keyless/local — set Provider 'ollama', Model to an installed tag e.g. 'llama3.1', ensure it is pulled and running at Ollama:Endpoint) or Anthropic (set Provider 'anthropic', Model e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8', and Anthropic:ApiKey or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). No model or key is hard-coded. When AI is enabled the source also needs a compliant Radar:Sec:UserAgent to fetch the EX-99.1 body. MaxInputLength caps the earnings-release characters sent to the analyzer; MinConfidence gates the directional read (below it -> no signal); MaxFilingsPerRun caps how many filings are analyzed per run.

Prices._comment: Daily price-history acquisition (REFERENCE/VALIDATION dataset only, AD-14). Price is NEVER evidence, NEVER a signal, NEVER a scoring input: it rides a dedicated seam (IPriceHistoryReader + data/prices/{ticker}.json) that is consumed by NOTHING in the scoring/evidence/signal/report path, and its acquisition runs OUTSIDE IRadarPipeline. DISABLED by default: Enabled=false means nothing price-related is registered and the pipeline graph is byte-for-byte unchanged. When Enabled=true the acquirer fetches daily bars for each seeded watch-universe ticker from the keyless Yahoo Finance chart endpoint (no key/secret/paid tier) over Range (one of 1d,5d,1mo,3mo,6mo,1y,2y,5y,10y,ytd,max) and append-merges them (deduped by Date, last-write-wins per date, ascending) into Directory. InterRequestDelaySeconds paces successive per-ticker reads (the endpoint is not per-IP throttled, so a small polite pace suffices). This exists for a FUTURE price-efficacy validation/backtest spec; it does not surface price in the report.
